fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Nevada Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Nevada — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license nevada, dental assistant certification nevada, RDA nevada, DANB CDA nevada, dental assistant radiography nevada

board.name: Nevada State Board of Dental Examiners

license_types.title: Dental Assistant / EFDA
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Nevada.

steps.title: Apply for State Credential
steps.description: Submit application to the Nevada State Board of Dental Examiners with required documentation and fees.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Dental Assistant / EFDA through the Nevada State Board of Dental Examiners by license.

faqs.question: Does Nevada require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Nevada requires Dental Assistant / EFDA through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Nevada?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Nevada.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Nevada?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Nevada?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $200. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Dental Assistant / EFDA through the Nevada State Board of Dental Examiners by license.
